title: Molecular contrastive learning of representations via graph neural networks

data_scope.pretraining_molecules: approximately 10 million unique unlabelled SMILES from PubChem
data_scope.data_split: scaffold split 80/10/10 for most tasks; random split for QM9

core_conclusion: MolCLR pre-trains GNN encoders on ~10 million unlabelled PubChem molecules via contrastive learning with three chemistry-aware graph augmentations (atom masking, bond deletion, subgraph removal). After fine-tuning, it surpasses all self-supervised baselines on 12/13 MoleculeNet benchmarks and matches or exceeds supervised SOTA on several tasks. The learned representations encode chemically meaningful molecular similarities without any labels.
why_it_matters: Breaks the label bottleneck in molecular ML; introduces three principled chemistry-aware graph augmentations; generalizes across property types (classification and regression) and scales; produces chemically interpretable transferable embeddings; enables drug discovery in data-scarce regimes.
why_nature: Large-scale empirical benchmark (10M pre-training, 13 tasks) sets a new reference; principled domain-aware augmentation contribution; direct societal relevance to drug discovery; fully reproducible via CodeOcean and GitHub.
added_value_to_my_research: Domain-aware graph augmentations (atom/bond/subgraph) as template for spatial network augmentations in GeoAI encoder pre-training
Pre-train/fine-tune paradigm justifies self-supervised spatial foundation models for scarce-label health accessibility settings
Scaffold-split OOD philosophy translates to geographic cross-validation that withholds entire regions for honest policy-transfer evaluation
Subgraph motif learning informs explainable spatial substructure attribution for identifying which neighbourhood subgraphs drive health access
Few-shot transfer from large unlabelled pre-training maps to telehealth / digital-divide analysis at sparse small-area level

figures.caption: Overview of MolCLR — pre-training pipeline, three graph augmentation strategies (atom masking, bond deletion, subgraph removal), and the pre-train/fine-tune framework
Augmentation ablation on classification benchmarks — subgraph removal (25% fixed ratio) achieves best average ROC-AUC; composition of all three augmentations hurts performance
t-SNE visualization of 100,000 PubChem validation molecules coloured by molecular weight — MolCLR clusters structurally similar molecules without labels
MolCLR representations vs. conventional fingerprints (ECFP, RDKFP) for a query molecule — cosine distance in MolCLR space correlates monotonically with chemical fingerprint similarity
